file.content:
tmc() {
  local name dir
  if [ "$CODESPACES" = "true" ]; then
    name="$RepositoryName"
    dir="/workspaces/$name"
  else
    name="$1"
    if [[ -z "$name" ]]; then
      echo "Usage: tmc <project-name>"
      return 1
    fi
    dir="$HOME/project/$name"
    if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
      echo "Directory not found: $dir"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  local session="team lead $name"
  # Attach if session already exists
  if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name"
    return
  fi
  tmux new -s "$session" -c "$dir" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name" \; send-keys "claude" Enter
}

input.old_string:
tmc() {
  local name dir
  if [ "$CODESPACES" = "true" ]; then
    name="$RepositoryName"
    dir="/workspaces/$name"
  else
    name="$1"
    if [[ -z "$name" ]]; then
      echo "Usage: tmc <project-name>"
      return 1
    fi
    dir="$HOME/project/$name"
    if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
      echo "Directory not found: $dir"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  local session="team lead $name"
  # Attach if session already exists
  if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name"
    return
  fi
  tmux new -s "$session" -c "$dir" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name" \; send-keys "claude" Enter
}
input.new_string:
tmc() {
  local names=() dirs=()
  if [ "$CODESPACES" = "true" ]; then
    names+=("$RepositoryName")
    dirs+=("/workspaces/$RepositoryName")
  else
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
      echo "Usage: tmc <project-name> [project-name ...]"
      return 1
    fi
    for name in "$@"; do
      local dir="$HOME/project/$name"
      if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
        echo "Directory not found: $dir"
        return 1
      fi
      names+=("$name")
      dirs+=("$dir")
    done
  fi
  local session="team lead ${names[*]}"
  local title="Claude Code: ${names[*]}"
  # Attach if session already exists
  if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "$title"
    return
  fi
  # Create session with first project
  tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -n "${names[0]}" -c "${dirs[0]}"
  tmux send-keys -t "$session:${names[0]}" "claude" Enter
  # Create additional windows for remaining projects
  for ((i=1; i<${#names[@]}; i++)); do
    tmux new-window -t "$session" -n "${names[$i]}" -c "${dirs[$i]}"
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:${names[$i]}" "claude" Enter
  done
  tmux set -t "$session" set-titles on
  tmux set -t "$session" set-titles-string "$title"
  tmux attach -t "$session"
}

tmc() {
  local names=() dirs=()
  if [ "$CODESPACES" = "true" ]; then
    names+=("$RepositoryName")
    dirs+=("/workspaces/$RepositoryName")
  else
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
      echo "Usage: tmc <project-name> [project-name ...]"
      return 1
    fi
    for name in "$@"; do
      local dir="$HOME/project/$name"
      if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
        echo "Directory not found: $dir"
        return 1
      fi
      names+=("$name")
      dirs+=("$dir")
    done
  fi
  local session="team lead ${names[*]}"
  local title="Claude Code: ${names[*]}"
  # Attach if session already exists
  if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "$title"
    return
  fi
  # Create session with first project
  tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -n "${names[0]}" -c "${dirs[0]}"
  tmux send-keys -t "$session:${names[0]}" "claude" Enter
  # Create additional windows for remaining projects
  for ((i=1; i<${#names[@]}; i++)); do
    tmux new-window -t "$session" -n "${names[$i]}" -c "${dirs[$i]}"
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:${names[$i]}" "claude" Enter
  done
  tmux set -t "$session" set-titles on
  tmux set -t "$session" set-titles-string "$title"
  tmux attach -t "$session"
}

export MONEYDEW="$HOME/project/moneydew"
export MDW="$MONEYDEW"
alias mdw='cd "$MONEYDEW"'
export MONEYDEWW="$MONEYDEW/webapp"
export MDWW="$MONEYDEWW"
alias mdww='cd "$MONEYDEWW"'
export MONEYDEWS="$MONEYDEW/service"
export MDWS="$MONEYDEWS"
alias mdws='cd "$MONEYDEWS"'

export SPLASHDOWN="$HOME/project/splashdown"
alias spl='cd "$SPLASHDOWN"'

export APPTORIAN="$HOME/project/apptorian"
export APPTORIAN_WEBAPP="${APPTORIAN}/webapp"
alias app='cd "${APPTORIAN}"'
alias appw='cd "${APPTORIAN_WEBAPP}"'

export CALYPSO="$HOME/project/calypso"
export CLP="$HOME/project/calypso"
export CLPW="$CALYPSO/webapp"
alias clp='cd "$CALYPSO"'
alias clpw='cd "$CALYPSO"/webapp'

# homebrew
[ -f /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew ] && eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv bash)"

#export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/project/utilscripts:/home/ketan/.yarn/bin:$HOME/.nvm"
export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/project/utilscripts"

export STORYBOOK_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1

alias coverage="w3m $MDWS/build/jacocoHtml/index.html"

function imgPort() {
# alternative approach

    if [ $# -ne 2 ]; then
        echo "Usage: imgPort <image name search> <port>"
        return 1
    fi
    SEARCH="$1"
    INNER_PORT="$2"

    CONTAINER_ID=`docker ps --filter ancestor="$SEARCH" --format '{{.ID}}'`
    if [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ]; then
        echo "Unable to find container matching \"$SEARCH\""
    fi

    OUTER_PORT=`docker inspect "$CONTAINER_ID" | jq -r ".[0].NetworkSettings.Ports[\"$INNER_PORT/tcp\"]|map(select(.HostIp==\"0.0.0.0\"))[0].HostPort"`

    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        return "$?"
    fi
    echo "$OUTER_PORT"
}

function testpg() {
    PORT=`imgPort postgres:15.1 5432`
    USERNAME="sa"
    DB_NAME="moneydew_db_test"
    PGPASSWORD=sa psql -h localhost --port "$PORT" --user "$USERNAME" --set=HISTFILE=~/.psql_history-testpg "$DB_NAME"
}

tmux-gui-refresh() {
    if ! command -v tmux >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "tmux not found"
        return 1
    fi
    if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
        echo "Not inside tmux"
        return 1
    fi

    eval "$(tmux show-environment -s | grep -E '^(export|unset) (DISPLAY|WAYLAND_DISPLAY|XAUTHORITY|XDG_RUNTIME_DIR|SSH_AUTH_SOCK|PULSE_SERVER)')"
}

if [ -n "$MDW_PROD_DATABASE_URL" ]; then
    alias mdwproddb='printf $CC_Orange1_FG ; printf $CC_DarkBlue_BG && psql --set=AUTOCOMMIT=OFF "$MDW_PROD_DATABASE_URL" && clear && printf ${RESET}'
fi

if [ -n "$CLPS_PROD_DATABASE_URL" ]; then
    alias clpsproddb='printf $CC_Orange1_FG ; printf $CC_DarkBlue_BG && psql --set=AUTOCOMMIT=OFF "$CLPS_PROD_DATABASE_URL" && clear && printf ${RESET}'
fi

alias vbd='vi "$DOTFILES/bashrc.dev-common.sh"'

export LOGDIR="$HOME/project/logs"
alias lf='cd "$LOGDIR"'
alias lfm='cd "$LOGDIR/moneydew"'



export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"  # This loads nvm
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion"  # This loads nvm bash_completion


export SKIP_YARN_COREPACK_CHECK=1 # https://github.com/yarnpkg/yarn/issues/9015

export WORKFLOW_PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/project/wkflw"
alias gwpi=git_worktree_project_init.py
alias gwt=git_worktree_create.py
alias rmgwt=git_worktree_close.py

alias bdcf='bd create --type feature'
alias bdcb='bd create --type bug'

export PATH="${PATH}:${WORKFLOW_PROJECT_DIR}/bin"

export BEADS_NO_DAEMON=1

function _bd_title() {
    local json
    if json=$(bd show "$1" --json 2>/dev/null); then
        echo "$json" | jq -r '.[0].title // empty'
    fi
}

function clb() {
    if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "Usage: clb <issue-key> [issue-key ...]"
        return 1
    fi

    printf '\033]0;CC: %s\007' "$*"
    if [ $# -eq 1 ]; then
        CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE=1 claude --worktree "$1" "work on issue $1"
    else
        CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE=1 claude "Work on the following issues in parallel using agent teams. Create a team and assign each issue to a separate teammate agent working in its own worktree. Issues: $*"
    fi
    printf '\033]0;\007'
}

function clready() {
    local ready_keys
    ready_keys=$(bd ready --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[].id' 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$ready_keys" ]; then
        echo "No ready issues found."
        return 1
    fi

    local keys_array=($ready_keys)
    echo "Ready issues (${#keys_array[@]}):"
    for key in "${keys_array[@]}"; do
        echo "  $key $(_bd_title "$key")"
    done
    echo ""

    echo "Starting Claude on ${#keys_array[@]} issue(s)..."
    clb "${keys_array[@]}"
}

tmc() {
  local name dir
  if [ "$CODESPACES" = "true" ]; then
    name="$RepositoryName"
    dir="/workspaces/$name"
  else
    name="$1"
    if [[ -z "$name" ]]; then
      echo "Usage: tmc <project-name>"
      return 1
    fi
    dir="$HOME/project/$name"
    if [[ ! -d "$dir" ]]; then
      echo "Directory not found: $dir"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  local session="team lead $name"
  # Attach if session already exists
  if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name"
    return
  fi
  tmux new -s "$session" -c "$dir" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name" \; send-keys "claude" Enter
}

. "$HOME/.cargo/env"

lines: -    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name"
+    tmux attach -t "$session" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "$title"
-  tmux new -s "$session" -c "$dir" \; set set-titles on \; set set-titles-string "Claude Code: $name" \; send-keys "claude" Enter
+  # Create additional windows for remaining projects

content.text:
The `tmc` function now accepts multiple project names. For example, `tmc foo bar baz` will:

1. Validate all project directories exist upfront (fails fast if any is missing)
2. Create a single tmux session with one window per project, each named after the project
3. Launch `claude` in each window
4. The session name and title reflect all projects (e.g., "team lead foo bar baz")

Single-project usage (`tmc myproject`) still works exactly as before.

f511d12 tmux.conf refresh SSH-related environment variables
43fa764 don't prompt when skipping dangerous permissions (mostly for agentsea)

67f36c8 swarm: add 20-task batch limit with context-reset continuation
